stem: A bag of flour weighs 4 pounds. There are 16 ounces in 1 pound. How many ounces does the bag of flour weigh?
explanation:
To convert pounds to ounces, multiply the number of pounds by 16 ounces per pound.

$$4 \text{ lb} \times \frac{16 \text{ oz}}{1 \text{ lb}} = 64 \text{ oz}$$

The pounds unit cancels, leaving ounces. The bag of flour weighs 64 ounces.

distractorRationale.A: Stops one step early: reports only the weight in pounds (4) without applying the conversion to ounces. The student records the given value instead of converting it.
distractorRationale.B: Wrong conversion factor: uses 12 ounces per pound instead of 16, giving $4 \times 12 = 48$ oz. A student may confuse avoirdupois ounces (16 per pound) with the troy system (12 troy ounces per troy pound), which is used for precious metals.
distractorRationale.D: Wrong conversion factor: uses 20 ounces per pound instead of 16, giving $4 \times 20 = 80$ oz. The student misremembers the conversion factor as 20 rather than the correct 16.
